CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, s1 VARCHAR(100), s2 VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (1, 'abcd', 'bc');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (2, 'hello world', 'world');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (3, 'test', 'testing');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (4, 'MonetDB', NULL);
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (5, NULL, 'test');
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES (6, NULL, NULL);
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 5;
SELECT contains(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE id = 6;
SELECT id, contains(s1, s2) AS result FROM test_strings WHERE result IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, t1.s1, t2.s2, contains(t1.s1, t2.s2) AS contains_result FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_strings t2 ON t1.id != t2.id;
SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_strings WHERE contains(s1, s2) IS TRUE) AS has_true_result;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE contains(s1, s2);
CREATE TABLE additional_strings (s1 VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO additional_strings VALUES ('abcdefgh');
INSERT INTO additional_strings VALUES ('xyz');
SELECT t.id, t.s1, a.s1 AS additional_s FROM test_strings t, additional_strings a WHERE contains(t.s1, a.s1);
SELECT t.id, t.s1, t.s2, CASE WHEN contains(t.s1, t.s2) THEN 'Included' ELSE 'Not Included' END AS status FROM test_strings t;
